LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA

January 29, 1990
Kathryn Donovan, Esg.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 958L4
Dear Kathy:
This letter constitutes a request for informal
written advice pursuant to Government Code S 83114(b).
This firm represents political 
committees
sponsored by corporations. From time to time, questions
arise concerning the status of such committees as ,,broad-
based" under the campaign contribution limitation provisions
of the Political Reform Act of L974r ds amended (the ,,Act',)
(Gov't Code SS 85100 et. seq. ) and regulations promulgated
thereunder by the Commission.
Chapter 5 of the Act defines a broad-based
political 
committee as a committee of persons which has:
(1) "been in existence for more than six monthsr,,
(2 ) "recelves eont-r-ibut-ions f rom on_e hundred. or nore
persons," and (3) "acting in concert makes contributions to
five or more candidates." Gov't Code S 851-02(d). Assuming
that the committee meets the ratter two requirements and it
files its statement of organization before it quarifies as a
recipient committee, our question is whether the six month
period commences on the date the Committee files its
Statement of Organization (i.e., the date it comes into
existence) or the date it receives contributions of $Lr000 or
more (i.e., the date it qualifies as a ,,committee,, under
section 8201-3(a) ).
The Secretary of State's office has informed us
that the six month period commences on the date that a
Statement of Organization is filed with that office.
Howeverr we understand that the Commission has informally
taken the position that a "committee" must both qualify as a


Kathryn Donovan Esq.
January 29, 1990
Page 2
lecipient committee under Government Code section 82013(a)
and register before the six month period commences.
Neither the Act nor the regulations directly
address this issue (See Gov't Code S 85102(d), 2 CaI .Code
Regs S 18502(a)(1)). Presumably, the Commission interprets
the definition of broad-based political 
committee by
reference to the definition of committee (Gov't Code
S 82013). Hov,ever, Government Code Section 85102
specifically defines "poLitical committee" and "broad-based
pcliticF.l co!0mittee" for purpose-e of Chagter 5 of the Act,
without reference to the definition provisions of Chapter 2
(cov't Code S 85102). Accordingly, we presume that to the
extent terms are defined by section 85102, such definitions
are the sole and exclusive definitions for purpose of Chapter
5.* Accordingly, we believe that the definitions of
political 
committee and broad-based political 
committee
supersede the definition of "comnittee" (Gov't Code
S 82013(a)) for purposes of Chapter 5.
If you have any questions, please do not hesitate
to contact me.
very truly yours ,
..Jt,/
--'t/-4 tt-z{,y{..1 rhz, t-
Teiesa Craigie Lahaderne
* 
For example, section 85102 defines ,,person.,. This
definition controls for purposes of Chapter 5. Accordingly,
section 85102 preenpts section 82047 which defines person
for other purposes of the Act.
10301493

sections.question: Assuming that the committee meets the latter two requirements and it files its statement of organization before it qualifies as a recipient committee, our question is whether the six month period commences on the date the Committee files its Statement of Organization (i.e., the date it comes into existence) or the date it receives contributions of $1,000 or more (i.e., the date it qualifies as a "committee" under section 82013(a)).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the six-month existence requirement for a broad-based political committee commences when a Statement of Organization is filed or when the committee qualifies as a recipient committee by receiving $1,000 or more in contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney (Teresa Craigie Lahaderne at Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro) to the FPPC General Counsel dated January 29, 1990. No FPPC response or conclusion sections are present. The letter clearly articulates the legal question and provides relevant statutory citations and background information.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Assuming that the committee meets the latter two requirements and it files its statement of organization before it qualifies as a recipient committee, our question is whether the six month period commences on the date the Committee files its Statement of Organization (i.e., the date it comes into existence) or the date it receives contributions of $1,000 or more (i.e., the date it qualifies as a "committee" under section 82013(a)).

embedding.summary: A law firm representing political committees sponsored by corporations requests informal written advice from the FPPC regarding when the six-month existence period begins for a committee to qualify as "broad-based" under the Political Reform Act's contribution limitation provisions.
